titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender, 13 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Bliss Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from sir Wilfrid Bliss Spender (1876-1960) to his wife, Lady Lillian Spender, née Dean, (1880-1968). In this letter Spender advises Lillian that future letters will be more seldom as the Division becomes more 'scurried'. He asks her to send him chocolate, and a shrapnel-proof steel waistcoat. The letter is unfinished.Sir Wilfrid Spender was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender wrote a collection of letters to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War. Lady Lillian served in the Ulster Volunteer Nursing Corps during the war, and was an organiser of the Ulster Division Comforts Fund.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
13th June My Pr We are likely to be more & more scurried
& later letters will very likely be impossible
for 3 or 4 days at a time so you must not be anxious about
things If you have time you might send out
some chocolate, the harder & more solid the
better. Someone the other day showed me a
steel waistcoat which is shrapnel proof. If
you should hear of such a thing you might send
it out soon. I could not see it advertised, but
the Government is beginning to turn them out
I believe & they might be useful in certain
eventualities tho' probably one would very seldom
this must have helped the entente.
